question: Additive trajectory_sha field on the hash-chained trial event (the task_commitment/rubric_sha256 precedent): approve the contract change with the absent-field-equals-pre-EVAL-12 compatibility posture?
author: claude/design

question: Dossier rendering technology: jinja2 templates + inline SVG charts with minimal inline JS (collapse/toggle only, zero external dependencies), or a JS charting layer for interactivity?
author: claude/design

question: LLM-generated narrative in the dossier: excluded by constraint in v1 (verdict layer is computed-only templates), or included as an EXPLORATORY-tagged optional layer?
author: claude/design

question: Verb surface: the dossier as an additional artifact of bench analyze (same fence, same findings_rendered event, no new verb), or a dedicated bench dossier verb?
author: claude/design

rationale: Decision session 2026-07-04: all EVAL-10/11/12 recommendations accepted. Additive trajectory_sha on the trial event per the task_commitment/rubric_sha256 precedent — binds the trajectory artifact to the chain so forensics reads evidence, not an editable log
migration: absent field = pre-EVAL-12 trial: no reader may require it; never refuse legacy chains
author: jyang

rationale: Accepted 2026-07-04. jinja2 is already a pinned dependency; inline SVG keeps the dossier byte-deterministic and archivable; inline JS is limited to collapse/toggle with zero external dependencies
author: jyang

rationale: Accepted 2026-07-04. A [judgment] generator inside an official artifact would launder judgment into computed claims; the verdict layer stays computed-only templates. An EXPLORATORY-tagged narrative layer may be raised as a new decision later
author: jyang

rationale: Accepted 2026-07-04. Riding bench analyze keeps one fence path, one findings_rendered event, and a flat verb surface; a dedicated verb would duplicate the fence and the one-event property
author: jyang
